from.subject: Re: Reminder: FedEx REST API Migration for PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App

ticket.subject: Re: [PluginHive] Re: Re: Reminder: FedEx REST API Migration for PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App
ticket.raw_subject: Re: [PluginHive] Re: Re: Reminder: FedEx REST API Migration for PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App
ticket.description:
This is a follow-up to your previous request #389508 "Re: Reminder: FedEx REST AP..."

Hello,

sorry for delay.
We have tested UK store dermahealer.co.uk (http://dermahealer.co.uk/)  using FedEx REST.
TEST ORDER: UK3945

1) OK - tested FedEx REST and looks like it is working OK (the same way as old FedEx API), meaning that I can decide which Fedex service the app should assigning as default to my orders. I can move the Fedex service up or down in "Automation Rules" menu setup.

2) Problem (2 PDF attached):
- Fedex International Priority ->  duplicated name and streetline shown on the label and invoice (same issue was on .com store, later fixed)
- FedEx Regional Economy -> duplicated name and streetline shown on the label and invoice (same issue was on .com store, later fixed)
Please discuss with your team (they fixed it in .com store)

Thanks
Arunas

When will old Fedex API stop working, any deadlines?

comments.body:
Hello Arunas,

Thank you for testing the UK store and sharing the label samples, that is very helpful.

Regarding the duplicated address issue: we have reviewed both attached PDFs and can confirm that "18 Hazel Grove Silsoe" does appear twice in the recipient address block on both the FedEx International Priority and FedEx Regional Economy labels for test order UK3945. We have noted that this was resolved for your .com store and will raise this with our team to apply the same fix to the UK store (dermahealer.co.uk). We will update you here once it has been addressed.

Regarding the FedEx legacy API deadline: FedEx officially retired its legacy SOAP/Web Services API on June 1, 2026, that deadline has already passed. Since you have already completed the migration to FedEx REST on the UK store, you are not affected and your labels and rates will continue to work normally.

We will be in touch once the duplicate address fix has been applied to the UK store. If you have any questions in the meantime, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Arunas,
 
Thank you for testing the UK store and sharing the label samples, that is very helpful.
 
Regarding the duplicated address issue: we have reviewed both attached PDFs and can confirm that "18 Hazel Grove Silsoe" does appear twice in the recipient address block on both the FedEx International Priority and FedEx Regional Economy labels for test order UK3945. We have noted that this was resolved for your .com store and will raise this with our team to apply the same fix to the UK store (dermahealer.co.uk). We will update you here once it has been addressed.
 
Regarding the FedEx legacy API deadline: FedEx officially retired its legacy SOAP/Web Services API on June 1, 2026, that deadline has already passed. Since you have already completed the migration to FedEx REST on the UK store, you are not affected and your labels and rates will continue to work normally.
 
We will be in touch once the duplicate address fix has been applied to the UK store. If you have any questions in the meantime, please do not hesitate to reach out.
 
Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Arunas,

Our development team has reviewed the reported behavior and confirmed that it requires a code update for the UK store.
The required update has been logged, and the team is currently working on it. We will keep you informed and update you as soon as it has been deployed to your Dermahealer UK store.

Thank you for your patience. We will keep you posted as soon as we have an update.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive
